data.title: China Mobile International activates SJC2 submarine cable, spanning 10,500km to boost connectivity in Asia-Pacific.
data.description: On July 16, 2025, China Mobile International Limited (CMI) announced the official activation of the Southeast Asia-Japan Cable 2 (SJC2) in Hong Kong. This new submarine cable system covers a distance of 10,500 kilometers and aims to transform connectivity in the Asia-Pacific region.

data.title: Copilot Agent Mode now available for JetBrains, Eclipse, and Xcode, providing advanced coding support in IDEs.
data.description: The widely used GitHub Copilot agent mode has been officially launched for JetBrains, Eclipse, and Xcode platforms, providing advanced autonomous coding features. This expansion will bring the powerful coding capabilities of Copilot to a larger number of developers.

data.title: Tom Lee highlights Michael Saylor-led firm's 35x surge due to Bitcoin and treasury strategy, stock soared from $13 to $450.
data.description: According to Wall Street strategist Tom Lee, the surge in Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ: MSTR) shares was not solely attributed to the rise in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) but also to strategic financial decisions. Five years ago, under the leadership of Michael Saylor, the stock soared from $13 to over $450 after adopting a Bitcoin treasury strategy. Out of the remarkable 35x increase, 11x was a result of Bitcoin's price surge from $11,000 to $118,000, with the remaining 25x increase attributed to the treasury strategy. Lee emphasized that the treasury strategy had a more significant impact than the token price gain. Lee's insights on the matter can be found in a post on DAT and BTC/ETH Treasuries with approval from @saylor. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Autel Europe and ON Power launch Europe’s first MaxiCharger Megawatt Charging System for heavy-duty transport, marking a key step in electrification.
data.description: Autel Europe has recently launched its first MaxiCharger Megawatt Charging System (MCS) in Europe, in collaboration with Icelandic CPO ON Power. This marks the debut of Autel’s MCS technology in the region, demonstrating a major advancement in the electrification of heavy-duty transport. The partnership with ON Power represents a crucial step in their plan to establish advanced infrastructure. This venture signifies a key milestone for Autel Europe, showcasing their progress in introducing innovative charging solutions to the European market, with a focus on enhancing sustainability in the transportation sector.

data.title: China-U.S. Youth explore Xi'an's living history with 25 delegates from both countries. Across the Pacific program continues.
data.description: On July 12, 2025, the second leg of the Across the Pacific: China-U.S. Youth Dialogue for a Shared Future program took place in Xi'an. Facilitated by ISC's 5th China-America Student Conference, the event hosted twenty-five delegates from both countries.

data.title: Statkraft to release Q2 2025 results on July 22, 2025, at 08:00 a.m. CEST.
data.description: Statkraft is set to announce their financial results for the second quarter of 2025 on Tuesday, July 22, 2025, at 8:00 a.m. Central European Summer Time (CEST). Investors and stakeholders can anticipate key insights into the company's performance during this period.

data.title: LEPAS debuts overseas at Indonesia Auto Show after unveiling in April 2025, part of global expansion plan.
data.description: In July 18, 2025, in Jakarta, Indonesia, Chery Group announced the launch of its new brand LEPAS, aiming to offer elevated lifestyle experiences on a global scale. The introduction of LEPAS marks a significant milestone in Chery Group's strategy to expand internationally. The brand is poised to enter the overseas market for the first time after being unveiled in April. With this move, Chery Group is looking to reach a wider audience and establish a presence beyond its domestic market.

data.title: 3M to report Q2 earnings 7/18. Analysts expect $2.01 EPS, $6.05B revenue vs $1.93 EPS, $6.25B revenue previous year.
data.description: 3M Company (NYSE: MMM) is set to unveil its second quarter earnings before the market opens on Friday, July 18. Analysts are predicting the company, headquartered in Saint Paul, Minnesota, to post earnings of $2.01 per share for the quarter, a slight increase from the $1.93 per share reported in the same period last year. Revenue projections for the quarter stand at $6.05 billion, down from $6.25 billion a year ago based on data from Benzinga Pro. The company's board announced a dividend of 73 cents per share for the second quarter on May 13. Closing at $159.04 on Thursday, 3M shares saw a 0.9% increase. For more details and the latest analyst ratings, readers can refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga's website.

data.title: Euroclear's H1 2025 results show solid performance despite challenges. Business income hit €932.
data.description: In Brussels, as of July 18, 2025, financial results for the first half year ending on June 30, 2025, were reported. Despite a difficult geopolitical and macroeconomic landscape, there was a strong business performance with an enhanced operating margin. The underlying business income for the first half of 2025 amounted to €932 million.

data.title: AI tools Grok and ChatGPT revolutionize crypto day trading, identifying sentiment changes swiftly for trading strategies.
data.description: Artificial intelligence technologies such as Grok and ChatGPT have revolutionized the way traders engage in crypto day trading. These tools analyze sentiment shifts in real time and convert them into organized trade strategies, making the process more efficient and data-driven for traders. This advanced technology is enabling traders to make well-informed decisions quickly, potentially resulting in higher profits and reduced risks associated with cryptocurrency trading.

data.title: PepsiCo's $2 billion healthy snacks portfolio shines, with focus on natural ingredients & no artificial additives. No-sugar drinks thrive ahead of protein beverage launch, Lay's & Tostitos to relaunch with cleaner options. Energy drink segment tops.
data.description: PepsiCo Inc. reported strong second-quarter earnings, focusing on a $2 billion permissible snack portfolio encompassing brands like SunChips and Simply, capitalizing on consumer preferences for natural ingredients. Chairman and CEO Ramon Laguarta highlighted their leadership in healthier snacking, with plans to relaunch Lay's and Tostitos by next fiscal year by removing artificial additives. PepsiCo is also expanding its no-sugar and energy drink segments, with success in no-sugar colas and growth in Gatorade and Propel in hydration. The energy drink category, bolstered by partnerships like Celsius and Starbucks, is an area of continued investment. The company is taking proactive steps to cater to evolving consumer demands.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller calls for 25 BPS cut in July due to sluggish economy; GDP growth at 1%. Trump pressures Powell.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller recently advocated for a 25 basis points cut in interest rates at the Federal Open Market Committee's July meeting. In a speech at New York University's Money Marketeers, Waller justified his position by citing recent economic data, stating that real GDP growth was likely around 1% in the first half of the year and is anticipated to remain soft throughout 2025. He also mentioned that consumer spending is projected to continue growing at a similar rate but may be dampened by a slowdown in real disposable income growth. Waller's remarks come amidst concerns about the labor market trends and their impact.
